evals.prompt: Create a module called `event_notifier` for a Drupal 10 site. The module should: (1) Define a custom service that checks whether notifications are enabled via a config object and returns a message. (2) Create a controller at /event-notifier/status that uses the service to display the notification status. The controller must inject the service via the create() pattern. (3) Implement hook_cron() in the .module file to log a message when cron runs. (4) The controller should handle the case where the config value might be NULL by accepting a nullable parameter in a helper method. Include proper error handling with try/catch. We want D11 compatibility.
evals.expected_output: A module with a service class, controller with DI, hook_cron implementation, and proper coding standards compliance
expectations: All classes (service class and controller class) have docblock comments with a summary line (/** * Description. */)
All public methods have docblock comments with @param and @return tags where applicable
hook_cron() implementation uses exactly `/** * Implements hook_cron(). */` with no @param or @return tags
No @file docblock in class files (src/*.php) -- @file docblocks only appear in .module or .install files if present
try/catch blocks use cuddled brace style: `} catch (` on the same line as the closing brace (NOT catch on a new line after the closing brace)
Any method parameter with a default value of NULL uses the ?type nullable syntax (e.g., ?string $value = NULL, NOT string $value = NULL)
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en event_notifier returns exit code 0
Generated module code passes phpcs --standard=Drupal,DrupalPractice static analysis without errors
